name: Index
description: Index Server — Enterprise instruction indexing platform for AI assistant governance

rules.rule: All exported functions and handlers must have unit tests
Test coverage must exceed 80% for src/services/ and src/server/
TypeScript strict mode must be enabled (tsc --noEmit must pass)
ESLint must report zero errors (warnings allowed)
All MCP tool handlers must follow the registerHandler() pattern in src/server/registry
Vitest tests must complete within the configured timeout (5s default per test)
Dispatcher action additions must include schema-contract tests verifying dispatch INPUT_SCHEMA properties are a superset of downstream handler schemas
Mutation actions routed through index_dispatch must have agent-perspective tests that construct params using only the dispatch schema (no pre-cooked wrappers or direct handler calls)
No secrets, API keys, or credentials in source code
Pre-commit hooks must be installed and active (scripts/pre-commit.ps1)
Mutation tools require INDEX_SERVER_MUTATION=1 and bootstrap gating
All environment configuration must flow through src/config/runtimeConfig.ts
Handler registration uses side-effect imports in src/services/toolHandlers.ts
Tool registry (INPUT_SCHEMAS, STABLE, MUTATION sets) must be updated for new tools
IndexContext is the single source of truth for instruction state (writeEntry + ensureLoaded)
Schema version must be tracked via src/versioning/schemaVersion.ts
Audit logging (logAudit) is required for all mutation operations
Instruction body size must be enforced at write-time: index_add and index_import must reject bodies exceeding bodyMaxLength with actionable split/cross-link guidance

Canonical seeds in seedBootstrap.ts must contain only generalized, public-safe content about the index server, Index operations, and bootstrapping — no environment-specific, org-specific, or custom configuration data
All features must begin as specifications in specs/
Version bumps follow semver and require CHANGELOG.md updates
Conventional commits required (feat:, fix:, test:, refactor:, docs:)
No automatic pushes — explicit user approval required
Instruction Index changes must pass governance hash validation
Documentation filenames use lowercase_underscore convention (e.g. project_prd.md, mcp_configuration.md)
All active repos MUST follow the dual-repo pattern: private dev repo for all development, public pub repo as read-only mirror
Public publication repos MUST NOT receive direct pushes; all updates MUST flow through the publish script (scripts/publish.cjs) from the dev repo
Internal artifacts (.specify/, specs/, issue templates, state/, logs/) MUST be excluded from publication via .publish-exclude
Publishes MUST be release-aligned; the publish script MUST be used (no ad-hoc file copies or direct pushes)
Public repos MUST have issues, wiki, and projects disabled; CONTRIBUTING.md MUST explain the contribution policy

Dev repos MUST have a pre-push hook that queries GitHub API for repo visibility and blocks pushes to public remotes; bypass requires a SHA-256 token computed by the publish scripts (PUBLISH_OVERRIDE=1 is NOT sufficient)
All tests MUST pass before commit
New features MUST include test coverage
Test count MUST NOT decrease below minTestCount threshold
Functional tests MUST validate full pipeline round-trips
Test coverage MUST be tracked and maintained above project threshold
Tests MUST be implemented, verified passing, and results stored before commit
TDD red/green is NON-NEGOTIABLE: tests MUST be written and verified FAILING before implementation code; failing test first, fix second (red-green-refactor)
Every bug fix MUST start with a failing regression test: write a test that reproduces the failure, then fix and verify pass; no fix without a red test first
Tests MUST exercise the REAL production code; toy reimplementations and stub copies of the code under test are prohibited; export internal functions if needed for testability
Server code MUST have tests; dependencies (I/O, network, external services) MUST be mocked but the code under test itself MUST NOT be mocked or replaced
Playwright e2e tests MUST cover dashboard UI, instruction CRUD lifecycle, and search functionality
Bug-prone handlers and complex code paths MUST have >= 5 test cases covering normal, edge, error, boundary, and concurrent scenarios
Source files SHOULD target <=600 lines; MUST NOT exceed 1000 (template literals exempt)
Each module MUST have a single primary responsibility; god-modules that mix unrelated concerns are prohibited
Source code MUST follow the project declared layered architecture; cross-layer imports MUST flow downward only
Circular dependencies are prohibited; module dependency graphs MUST be acyclic
Dead code MUST be removed before commit; unused imports, variables, and unreachable branches are not allowed
Error handling MUST be explicit; swallowed exceptions (empty catch blocks) are prohibited — errors MUST be logged, re-thrown, or handled with documented rationale
Public APIs MUST have JSDoc documentation including @param, @returns, and @throws where applicable
Build MUST succeed with zero errors before commit
No hardcoded secrets or credentials in source
Dependencies MUST be pinned to exact versions in lock files
All stateful data MUST persist to disk; startup MUST auto-restore
Corrupted or empty files MUST result in safe empty state, not crashes
Schema changes to persisted files MUST include migration logic or be backwards-compatible
PII pre-commit hooks MUST be installed and active; .pre-commit-config.yaml with detect-secrets and detect-private-key MUST be present
.secrets.baseline MUST be committed and kept current; new secrets MUST be audited before allowlisting
Committed source, data files, and test fixtures MUST NOT contain real PII; use synthetic data for tests
